ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for geoduck clam stewed in superior stock. FOODON_03302050 (clam food product) is the closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJK (Clams and cockles) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for geoduck clam prepared in superior stock broth.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for geoduck clam stewed in superior stock as a prepared dish.

main_ingredients: superior stock (chicken broth, pork bones, Jinhua ham, dried scallop)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of geoduck clam stewed in superior stock is approximately 250 g, served as a shared dish at a zi char or seafood restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Clam, mixed species, cooked, moist heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15157); adapted with superior stock broth contribution estimated from USDA FoodData Central chicken broth entry (NDB 06116) and IMR Malaysia MyFCD shellfish entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / IMR Malaysia
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Geoduck clam (Panopea generosa) is not individually listed in USDA SR Legacy or IMR MyFCD. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked clam (NDB 15157) as the closest approved shellfish analogue, with sodium and energy adjusted upward to reflect superior stock (a rich seasoned broth containing chicken, pork, Jinhua ham, and dried scallop). Protein and fat values are anchored to the cooked clam baseline. The adaptation is conservative and documented.

gi_source.title: Shellfish and seafood GI — analogous estimate based on mixed shellfish / seafood entries; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for geoduck clam stewed in superior stock. Shellfish and lean seafood dishes are consistently low-GI foods due to negligible starch content. GI estimated at 25 based on analogous shellfish entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (25 × 4.8 × 2.5) / 100 = 3.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium from superior stock broth
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be cautious with this dish as the superior stock contributes approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g; a 250 g serving delivers ~1050 mg sodium, representing nearly half the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg, and portion control or requesting reduced-sodium broth is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Geoduck clam stewed in superior stock has a low glycemic index (~25) and a high protein content (~14.5 g per 100 g), making it a suitable protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments; the low glycemic load of approximately 3 per serving supports stable postprandial glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load from broth versus beneficial omega-3 fatty acids and low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While geoduck clam provides beneficial polyunsaturated fatty acids and low saturated fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), the sodium content of the superior stock (~420 mg per 100 g) poses a concern for cardiovascular disease management, and individuals should limit portion size and request lower-sodium preparation where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Geoduck Clam Stewed in Superior Stock is a premium Cantonese-influenced zi char and Malaysian Chinese seafood dish featuring geoduck clam (象拔蚌, Panopea generosa), a large, long-necked bivalve mollusc prized for its sweet, firm, and slightly crunchy texture. The clam is cleaned, sliced, and gently stewed or briefly poached in superior stock — a rich, deeply flavoured Chinese master broth made by slow-simmering chicken carcasses, pork bones, Jinhua ham, and dried scallops, seasoned with Shaoxing rice wine, ginger, garlic, and light soy sauce. The resulting dish is delicate, umami-rich, and subtly sweet, with the clam retaining a pleasant bite. Nutritionally, geoduck clam is an excellent source of high-quality protein (~14.5 g per 100 g), vitamin B12 (~22 mcg per 100 g, far exceeding daily requirements), iron (~13.5 mg per 100 g), selenium, and zinc. The fat content is low (~2.2 g per 100 g) with a favourable polyunsaturated-to-saturated ratio. The primary clinical concern is sodium from the superior stock (~420 mg per 100 g), which is relevant for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The glycemic index is low (~25), making this dish appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. In Singapore and Malaysia, this dish is commonly served at zi char stalls and seafood restaurants as a shared centrepiece, often priced as a premium item. It is associated with celebratory dining and is popular in Cantonese, Hokkien, and Malaysian Chinese culinary traditions.

confidence_reason: Geoduck clam (Panopea generosa) is not individually listed in USDA SR Legacy, IMR MyFCD, or HPB Singapore Foodo as a prepared dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked mixed-species clam (NDB 15157) as the closest approved shellfish analogue, with sodium adjusted upward to reflect the superior stock broth. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database shellfish entries. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation is anchored to a well-characterised approved source and the adjustments are conservative and documented.
notes: 1. The input food name 'Gooduck Clam Sorved with Superior Stock' contains two apparent typographical errors: 'Gooduck' (should be 'Geoduck') and 'Sorved' (should be 'Stewed' or 'Served'). The canonical name has been corrected to 'Geoduck Clam Stewed in Superior Stock' based on the dish description and common restaurant usage. 2. Geoduck clam (Panopea generosa) is not present as a standalone entry in USDA SR Legacy, IMR MyFCD, or HPB Singapore Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked clam (NDB 15157) with sodium adjusted upward (~420 mg per 100 g) to reflect the superior stock contribution. 3. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy clam entry used as the source analogue. 4. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy clam entry used as the source analogue. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy cooked clam data; shellfish do not contain industrially produced trans fats). 6. Dietary fibre is 0.0 (confirmed zero; bivalve molluscs contain no dietary fibre). 7. GI is an analogous estimate of 25 based on University of Sydney GI Database shellfish entries and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). 8. This dish is not halal-certified as superior stock typically contains pork-derived ingredients (pork bones, Jinhua ham). 9. The cuisine tag 'Crab' in the input appears to be a field survey platform categorisation error; this dish features geoduck clam, not crab.
